concept.title: Postman Overview
concept.shortdesc: Postman is an API platform that enables developers and technical writers to design, test, and document APIs in a centralized workspace.

conbody.p: Postman provides a graphical interface for sending HTTP requests, inspecting responses, and automating API tests. You can use Postman as a desktop application on Windows, macOS, and Linux, or as a web app in a supported browser.

Postman organizes API work into collections, which are groups of related requests. Collections allow you to structure your API tests logically, share them with team members, and generate documentation automatically.

section.title: Key Capabilities
section.p: Postman supports the following core capabilities:

ul.li: API Testing — Send HTTP requests and validate responses using built-in test scripts written in JavaScript.
Environment Management — Define variables for different environments such as development, staging, and production, and switch between them without editing requests.
Collection Runner — Execute all requests in a collection in sequence to automate regression and integration testing.
API Documentation — Generate human-readable API documentation directly from your collections and publish it to a public or private URL.
Mock Servers — Simulate API endpoints to test integrations before the API is fully developed.
Team Collaboration — Share collections, environments, and workspaces with team members using Postman workspaces.

section.title: Postman Plans
section.p: Postman offers the following account types:

ul.li: Free — Includes core API testing and basic documentation features with limited team collaboration.
Basic — Adds enhanced collaboration, more mock server calls, and custom domains for published documentation.
Professional — Includes advanced team features, audit logs, and priority support.
Enterprise — Provides SSO, dedicated support, and enterprise-grade security controls.

section.p: The procedures in this guide apply to the Free and Basic plans unless otherwise noted.

ul.li: A Postman account. Create a free account at postman.com.
Postman installed on your system, or access to the Postman web app.
Basic familiarity with HTTP request methods and API concepts.
